mainEntity.name: What are mention rate, citation rate, and positioning in AI share of voice, and why do they matter?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Mention rate counts how often your brand appears in AI answers; citation rate tracks how often you’re linked or referenced as a source; positioning measures how prominently you’re recommended versus competitors. Together, they give a richer view of AI share of voice.

Context: A brand might appear frequently but only in low-importance contexts, or be cited but not clearly recommended. Looking at all three metrics helps you understand real influence, not just presence.

Key factors:
- Mention rate: frequency of brand name appearing
- Citation rate: links or explicit source references
- Positioning: where and how you’re recommended vs rivals
- Comparative share: your proportion of all mentions in your sector

Practical guidance: Track mention rate first, then add citation and positioning scoring as you mature your program. Use mismatches (e.g., high mentions, low citations) to prioritize next improvements.

Soft positioning: Treat these metrics as layers that turn raw AI presence into an actionable visibility profile.
acceptedAnswer.description: Mention rate is how often your brand appears; citation rate is how often you’re referenced or linked as a source; positioning is how prominently you’re recommended versus competitors. Tracking all three gives a fuller picture of your influence in AI answers.
